methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Bedrock Robotics, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company, currently carries a $1.75B post-money valuation following $270M in total fundraising. The valuation assessment relies on a peer universe of nine comparable companies: one public entity (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private firms, with five primary stage-matched comparables in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector.

Two valuation methods triangulate the company's fair value. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $1.8B last post-money, implying $3.3B. The peer-median approach evaluates five same-sector, same-stage companies—1X Technologies ($10.0B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), Fairmat ($350M), and GrayMatter Robotics ($300M)—yielding a median of $500M, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $425M (low) to $3.8B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The current $1.75B valuation sits near the lower bound of this range. Primary uncertainty stems from the extreme dispersion within peer valuations: 1X Technologies trades at a 20x premium to the peer median, while GrayMatter trades at 0.6x. This heterogeneity reflects material differences in technology maturity, capital efficiency, and market positioning across the robotics subsector. Confidence in the valuation range depends critically on clarifying Bedrock's relative positioning against these outlier comparables.
